title: Jet Ski Rental Naples 2026: Costs, Routes & Where to Hire
html:
Naples offers some of the most exhilarating jet ski rental experiences in the Mediterranean, with departures from Molo Beverello and prices starting around €120 for a one-hour solo ride. Whether you're a first-timer or a seasoned rider, you can book through BoatHire24 and compare verified listings instantly.

How much does it cost to rent a jet ski in Naples?

Prices range from €80 for a 30-minute taster to €350 for a two-hour guided tour. Basic solo rentals start at €120 per hour, with double-seat jet skis costing around €160. For a half-day excursion, expect to pay €250, €400. The best way to compare current prices is to check boat rental Naples listings on BoatHire24, which include real-time availability and customer reviews.

Where to set off in Naples

The most common departure point is Molo Beverello, the main ferry terminal in the city centre. Other popular launch sites include the Port of Pozzuoli to the west and the Marina di Stabia in Castellammare di Stabia. From these harbours, you can ride along the coast towards the islands of Ischia and Procida, or explore the Bay of Naples as far as the Sorrento peninsula. The scenic route to the Castel dell'Ovo is a favourite for short trips, while longer journeys take you past the underwater archaeological park of Gaiola.

Do you need a licence to rent a jet ski in Naples?

In Italy, no licence is required for jet skis with engines up to 50 horsepower. For more powerful models, a patente nautica (boating licence) is mandatory. Rental operators always confirm exact requirements before booking, and most offer a brief safety briefing. First-timers should ask about supervised sessions that include a guide.

Tip: book early in the morning (around 9am) to avoid afternoon crowds and catch the calmest sea conditions in the Gulf of Naples.

Which boat to rent, from jet skis to yachts

If you're after more than a jet ski, BoatHire24 also lists a wide range of boats. The the Mefasa 90 listing is a top choice for a luxurious day charter. Here is a quick guide to popular rental options:

  1. Jet ski, best for thrill-seekers, 2 persons max, from €120 per hour.
  2. Small motorboat (e.g. Olympic 490), ideal for couples, 4 persons, from €150 half-day.
  3. Catamaran (Lagoon 42), spacious for families, 8, 10 persons, from €800 full-day.
  4. Luxury yacht (Mefasa 90), crewed charter, 12 guests, from €5,000 per day.
  5. Inflatable RIB, versatile and fast, 6 persons, from €200 half-day.

Cost breakdown for Naples boat rental

  • Shared jet ski tour (1 hour): €80 per person, including guide and life jacket.
  • Private jet ski (30 min solo): €70 per jet ski.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €250 with fuel deposit.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,200 plus skipper fee.
  • Crewed luxury yacht (Mefasa 90) for 8 guests: €6,500 including lunch.

At a glance

  • Price band: €70, €6,500
  • Peak months: July, August, early September
  • Typical trip length: 1 hour to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 1, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Naples

Can I rent a jet ski in Naples without a licence?

Yes, for models under 50 hp, which covers most rental fleets for tourists.

What is the best time of day for jet skiing in Naples?

Early morning (8am, 10am) offers flat water and less boat traffic in the Gulf.

Is it safe to ride a jet ski from Naples to Ischia?

Yes, the 18-nautical-mile crossing is feasible on a full tank, but a guide is strongly advised due to shipping lanes.

Do I need to book in advance for a jet ski in Naples?

Yes, especially in July and August, when demand is high; last-minute availability is limited.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Naples and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
